test.notes: Contract suite passed, root node tests passed (136/136), and browser-use-discovery tests passed (69/69).

typecheck.notes: All listed JS entrypoints passed node --check syntax validation.

blockingIssues.description: The rerun fixed the untracked tests, innocuous placeholder literals, and missing-sheetId regression coverage, but the feature still does not land the claimed OAuth/session and local service boundary hardening in its own commit set. Review evidence ties the boundary code in app.js and server/security-boundaries.mjs to earlier non-feature commits rather than the feature-owned commits ac86145 and 43b8226.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update the skill to acknowledge the mission-approved dirty-baseline and temporary-port verification exceptions when AGENTS.md or .factory/library guidance explicitly permits them.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The latest security-boundary-hardening handoff had to mark followedProcedure=false because it validated on port 3947 while port 3847 was occupied and preserved unrelated dirty-baseline artifacts; both behaviors are already explicitly allowed by mission AGENTS.md and .factory/library/user-testing.md, but the integration-refactor-worker skill still describes only the canonical-port/cleaner path.

rejectedObservations.observation: Isolated-worktree validation still requires manually copying integrations/browser-use-discovery/state/worker-config.json.
